name: Chicken Pie with Biscuit Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, bring the chicken broth to a boil.
2. Add carrots, potatoes, and celery to the boiling broth. Simmer for 10-15 minutes or until vegetables are tender. Transfer veggies to a bowl and set aside; reserve broth.
3. In another saucepan, cook the onion in butter over medium-low heat, stirring, until softened.
4. Gradually add flour to the onions, stirring constantly, until a smooth roux forms.
5. Slowly whisk in 3 cups of reserved broth into the roux. Bring mixture to a boil and whisk continuously.
6. Add thyme, nutmeg, parsley, salt, and pepper to taste. Stir gently to combine.
7. Pour the sauce over chicken and veggies in the bowl; stir just to mix everything.
8. Spoon the filling into a 2-quart baking dish.
9. In a large bowl, sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
10. Add cubed butter and shortening; work mixture with hands until it resembles coarse meal.
11. Gradually add grated cheese to the dry ingredients; toss to mix.
12. Beat egg and buttermilk in a measuring cup until combined; set aside.
13. Pour the wet mixture into the flour mixture, stirring just until a dough forms.
14. Gather the dough into a ball on a floured surface; knead briefly.
15. Roll the dough to 1/2-inch thickness. Cut rounds using a 2-inch fluted biscuit cutter, pressing lightly in a dish.
16. Place the biscuits over the chicken mixture.
17. Beat egg yolk with milk and brush tops of the biscuits with the wash.
18. Prick the biscuits all over with a fork.
19. Bake in a preheated 450°F oven until biscuits are puffed and golden, about 15-25 minutes, or until filling is bubbling.
